brief_document.title: Fix README status, add aion_flow README, and root-level getting started
brief_document.purpose: The root README says 'implementation not yet started' (false — 135+ briefs are landed and working). The aion_flow SDK has no README. There are no root-level prerequisites or getting-started docs. New developers either think the project isn't built or can't find where to start.
brief_document.task: Update the root README status to reflect reality. Write a README for the aion_flow Gleam SDK explaining the API, types, and how to write a workflow. Add a root-level GETTING-STARTED.md with prerequisites and a quickstart path.

requirements.title: Fix root README status section
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL update the root README's Status section to accurately reflect: engine complete, server functional, worker SDKs available, three working examples. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT claim features that don't work (e.g. don't claim dashboard is functional if it's an empty shell).
acceptance: Status section says the engine is implemented and functional.
Status section lists what works and what's in progress honestly.
No claim of 'implementation not yet started' exists anywhere.

requirements.title: Write aion_flow SDK README
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL create a README.md in the aion_flow Gleam SDK directory explaining: what it is, how to define a workflow, how to declare activities, how to use signals/timers/queries, and a minimal example. THE SYSTEM SHALL reference the hello-world example for a complete working sample.
acceptance: README explains the core API: workflow definition, activity calls, signals.
README includes a minimal code example.
README links to the hello-world example for a full walkthrough.

requirements.title: Root-level GETTING-STARTED.md
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL create a GETTING-STARTED.md at the repo root listing prerequisites (Rust, Gleam, Python for workers), the quickstart path (hello-world example), and links to deeper documentation. THE SYSTEM SHALL be copy-pasteable — a developer can go from clone to running workflow in the listed steps.
acceptance: Prerequisites listed: Rust toolchain, Gleam, Python (optional for workers).
Steps to run hello-world are complete and copy-pasteable.
Links to examples/, docs/API.md, and aion_flow README.

boundaries: SHALL NOT rewrite the entire README — only fix the Status section and add links.
SHALL NOT document features that don't work yet (dashboard, some SDK features).
SHALL NOT duplicate the hello-world README — link to it instead.
verification: Root README Status section is accurate.
No broken links in any of the documents.
